Patch Set 4:

Hi Andreas, Sébastien, and Yingjin. This patch is #3 in the multi-fileset patch series. This patch focuses on setting up the in-memory data structure for alternate filesets to synchronize with the nodemap IAM. This patch does not use this data structure yet, and it is included in the following patch.

Whenever you have some time, could you please review it?
Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

(3 comments)

Patch Set 4:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 4:

(3 comments)

Thank you both for the reviews. I will include your comments on refresh.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

Giving this a -1 so that it isn't landed right now due to a potential kernel panic after the rb tree is destroyed and new items are added to it. 

Further, due to some changes, some patches do not seem to be cherry-pickable anymore. I'm investigating and will probably rebase parts of the patch series accordingly, including all the remaining comments.
Uploaded patch set 5.

Patch Set 5:

(3 comments)

Change log:
- Rebased the patch since it was no longer cherry-pickable due to recent changes in the nodemap IAM patch.
- Included the previous comments (also, renamed alternate fileset structs and functions to fileset_alt_*)
- Fixed a potential panic when filesets are re-added after the tree was destroyed in "fileset_alt_destroy_tree()" in "nodemap_fileset_alt.c"

parents.subject: LU-18357 ptlrpc: Add fileset ID handling on the nodemap IAM

commit.subject: LU-18357 ptlrpc: Add alternate fileset data structure handler
commit.message:
LU-18357 ptlrpc: Add alternate fileset data structure handler

Handling multiple future alternate filesets requires an in-memory data
structure that is synchronized with the nodemap IAM storage for
filesets. Specifically, it is necessary to properly handle gaps in the
cluster idx subid range that is reserved for filesets which occur when
filesets are deleted.

To easily synchronize the IAM filesets with the in-memory nodemap, an
this patch implements an ordered associative array, rb tree in this
case, which is naturally ordered by a node key - in this case the
global fileset id that can later be directly mapped to the IAM cluster
idx subid range for filesets.

This patch sets the maximum number of alternate filesets to 255 (+1
primary fileset).

This patch only includes the management of the fileset rb tree and
includes all necessary functions. The next patch connects the rb tree
to the IAM and add the API for adding and deleting filesets.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Test-Parameters: testlist=conf-sanity env=ONLY=134
Test-Parameters: testlist=sanity-hsm env=ONLY=411
Test-Parameters: testlist=sanity-quota env=ONLY=75
Test-Parameters: testlist=sanity-sec
Test-Parameters: testlist=sanity-selinux env=ONLY=21
